universities.name: Imperial College London
universities.model_note: Offers expressed as specific A-Level grade combinations (e.g. A*A*A).

universities.name: University of Cambridge
universities.entry_model: UK fixed grade tariff + STEP/subject tests
universities.model_note: Among the most academically demanding offers in the world; Further Maths is the stated normal minimum for Mathematics.

universities.name: University College London (UCL)
universities.model_note: Offers expressed as specific A-Level grade combinations.

universities.name: University of Manchester
universities.model_note: Offers expressed as specific A-Level grade combinations; alternative routes via Maths + Further Maths.

universities.name: London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE)
universities.model_note: A* in Maths is essential for most quantitative programmes; Further Maths is desirable but not always required.

universities.name: University of Warwick
universities.model_note: Data Science offer drops from A*A*A* to A*AA if you present Maths + Further Maths.

universities.name: University of Leeds
universities.model_note: Actuarial Mathematics offers an AAB/A*BB route that opens up with AS Further Maths.

universities.name: University of Southampton
universities.model_note: Offers expressed as specific A-Level grade combinations.

universities.name: City St George's, University of London (Bayes Business School)
universities.model_note: Bayes Business School requires an A in Maths for Actuarial Science.

universities.name: SOAS, University of London
universities.model_note: Typically ABB-AAB; Urdu/regional-language background is an asset for South Asian Studies, not a requirement.

universities.name: Queen Mary University of London
universities.model_note: AAA including an A in Maths for Actuarial Science.

universities.name: National University of Sciences & Technology (NUST)
universities.entry_model: Composite score (academic record + NUST entry test)
universities.model_note: Maths is mandatory for CS/Economics/Accounting & Finance; Pre-Engineering group (Maths+Physics) required for all engineering disciplines.

universities.name: Lahore University of Management Sciences (LUMS)
universities.entry_model: Composite score (academic record + SAT/LCAT-style test + interview)
universities.model_note: Pakistan's top-ranked institution for business and management education (QS Subject 2026: 101-150 band).

universities.name: Institute of Business Administration (IBA), Karachi
universities.entry_model: Composite score (A-Level average + IBA aptitude test + interview)
universities.model_note: Typical A-Level requirement: average of 1 B + 2 C grades; ranked 151-250 globally for Business & Economics (QS Subject 2026).

universities.name: Federal Urdu University of Arts, Sciences & Technology (FUUAST), Karachi
universities.model_note: A specialist Urdu-medium-friendly public university with dedicated Urdu and Mass Communication departments.

universities.name: National University of Modern Languages (NUML), Islamabad
universities.entry_model: Merit-based; A-Level accepted with minimum 50% marks
universities.model_note: Specialist languages-focused public university with strong Media & Communication Studies programmes.

universities.name: University of Toronto
universities.model_note: Requires 3 A-Levels + 5 IGCSE/O-Levels; Maths AS/A-Level needed wherever a Calculus prerequisite applies (most STEM/business programmes). SAT/ACT optional.

universities.name: New York University (NYU)
universities.model_note: Accepts A-Levels directly for international applicants; recommends A-Level Maths for Stern Business/CS/Engineering, and Physics+Chemistry+Calculus for Tandon Engineering.

universities.name: University of Melbourne
universities.model_note: Accepts A-Levels directly; minimum 3 A-Levels with at least a C grade in prerequisite subjects; publishes a guaranteed entry score per A-Level equivalent.

universities.name: American University of Sharjah (AUS)
universities.model_note: Minimum required average is roughly 85% in the final year of secondary school (or best two of the last three years); A-Levels are recognised via equivalency.
